message.abstract:
<jats:title>Summary</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>BACKGROUND</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>The outbreak of a novel coronavirus (SARS-CoV-2, previously provisionally named 2019 novel coronavirus or 2019-nCoV) since December 2019 in Wuhan, China, has become an emergency of major international concern. Apart from the respiratory system, it is unclear whether SARS-CoV-2 can also directly infect other tissues such as the kidney or induce acute renal failure.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>METHODS</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    We conducted a retrospective analysis of estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) along with other clinical parameters from 85 patients with laboratory-confirmed COVID-19 admitted to a hospital in Wuhan from January 17, 2020 to March 3, 2020. Kidney tissues from six patients with postmortem examinations were analyzed by Hematoxylin and Eosin (H&E) and
                    <jats:italic>in situ</jats:italic>
                    expression of viral nucleocaspid protein (NP) antigen, immune cell markers (CD8, CD68 and CD56) and the complement C5b-9 was detected by immunohistochemistry. Moreover, the viral particles in kidneys were also investigated by transmission electronic microscope (EM).
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>RESULTS</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    27.06% (23/85) patients exhibited acute renal failure (ARF). The eldery patients and cases with comorbidities such as hypertension and heart failure more easily developed ARF (65.22%
                    <jats:italic>vs</jats:italic>
                    24.19%,
                    <jats:italic>p</jats:italic>
                    < 0.001; 69.57% vs 11.29%,
                    <jats:italic>p</jats:italic>
                    < 0.001, respectively). H&E staining demonstrated kidney tissues from postmortems have severe acute tubular necrosis and lymphocyte infiltration. Immunohistochemistry showed that SARS-CoV-2 NP antigen was accumulated in kidney tubules. EM observation also demonstrated that viruses-like particles are visible in the kidneys. Viral infection not only induces CD68
                    <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                    macrophages infiltrated into tubulointerstitium, but also enhances complement C5b-9 deposition on tubules.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>CONCLUSIONS</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    SARS-CoV-2 induces ARF in COVID-19 patients. Viruses directly infect human kidney tubules to induce acute tubular damage. The viruses not only have direct cytotoxicity, but also initiate CD68
                    <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                    macrophage together with complement C5b-9 deposition to mediate tubular pathogenesis.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: Human Kidney is a Target for Novel Severe Acute Respiratory Syndrome Coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2) Infection
